description: Export the contents of a plot to a file. Can create hardcopy images, convert to different file formats, or create data files to recreate the plot. If no plot name is specified, the current view is assumed. Default filename is 'flac3d_name' where 'name' is the plot name.

python_sdk_alternative.workaround: itasca.command('plot export bitmap filename <path>') - no direct Python SDK method

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: renders the plot to a bitmap file. The following keywords can be used to control the format and size of the bitmap created: sets the dots per inch setting in the resulting file (if applicable). The default will be based on the current video display. sets the dots per meter setting in the resulting file (if applicable). The default will be based on the current video display. sets the destination file name. If one is not specified, then one will be assigned. The extension determines the format, and PNG is used by default if no extension is specified. PNG, BMP, PPM, XBM, and XPM formats are supported. determines the size of the bitmap (in pixels). The default is 1024 x 768.

keywords.name: csv
keywords.description: creates a text file of data in CSV (Comma Separated Values) format, with data data from all plot items that support the conversion (e.g., charts, tables, etc.). This will be arranged in tabular form. This file can be easily imported into a spreadsheet progral like Excel, for further processing. The filename keyword can be used to specify the file output, which will have the CSV extension by default.

keywords.name: datafile
keywords.description: This command exports a data file that, if read with the program call command, should completely recreate the contents of the plot. The filename keyword can be used to specify the file output, which will have the F3DAT extension by default.

keywords.name: dxf
keywords.description: This command attempts to export the geometric contents of the plot as a DXF file. NOTE: The DXF format does not support all of the features used to generate a plot. Your results may differ substantially from the plot on the screen. The filename keyword can be used to specify the file output, which will have the DXF extension by default.

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: creates a PDF file of the plot. sets the destination file name. The default extension will be PDF. specifies use of the landscape layout. The default is portrait. specifies the amount of margin to leave around the edges of the page in the layout (in inches or millimeters). The default is 0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25 in. specifies the paper size to use in the layout (in inches or millimeters). The default is 11.0, 8.5 in. determines the size of the bitmap used to render the 3D image. By default, the current print image size setting is used. See the PRINT SIZE property in the PLOT global settings dialog and the plot print-size command.

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: creates a Postscript file of the plot. sets the destination file name. The default extension will be PS. specifies use of the landscape layout. The default is portrait. specifies the amount of margin to leave around the edges of the page in the layout (in inches or millimeters). The default is 0.25, 0.25, 0.25, 0.25 in. specifies the paper size to use in the layout (in inches or millimeters). The default is 11.0, 8.5 in. determines the size of the bitmap used to render the 3D image. By default, the current print image size setting is used. See the PRINT SIZE property in the PLOT global settings dialog and the plot print-size command.

keywords.name: print
keywords.description: sends the plot to the operating system print system. If in interactive mode, this will open up a print dialog. If running as part of a data file, the plot will be sent to the default windows printer.

keywords.name: size
keywords.description: generates a Scalable Vector Graphics (SVG) file of the plot. The following keywords are available: specifies which file and path to give to the SVG file. The default extension is SVG. specifies the size of the bitmap portion of the plot (in pixels). By default, the current print image size setting is used. See the PRINT SIZE property in the PLOT global settings dialog and the plot print-size command.

examples.description: Export current plot to PNG file
Export plot 'myplot' to PDF in landscape orientation
Export plot as SVG vector graphics
Export plot data to CSV file
Export plot as high-resolution 300 DPI PNG

keywords.name: filename
keywords.description: generates a VRML file representing the plot. This file can be viewed directly by a compatible application. For example, Adobe Acrobat 3D can be used to turn the resulting VRML into a PDF file, or to embed the VRML file into another PDF document. The following keywords are available: specifies whether the VRML file output will be compressed or in plain text. The default is false. specifies which file and path to give to the VRML file. The default extension is WRL.

notes: For detailed plot item keywords (color-by, cut, transparency, legend), use pfc_browse_reference("plot-items") to list item types and pfc_browse_reference("plot-items ball") for full syntax.
